zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Beauchamp於1404年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾時，她的父親Richard Beauchamp23歲，母親Elizabeth de Berkeley18歲。她於1425年9月6日與Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1467年6月14日在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 包利斯 凱德拉爾享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Beauchamp于1404年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾时，她的父亲Richard Beauchamp23岁，母亲Elizabeth de Berkeley18岁。她于1425年9月6日与Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1467年6月14日在英国英格兰倫敦聖 包利斯 凱德拉爾享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Beauchamp was born in 1404, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Beauchamp, was 23 and her mother, Elizabeth de Berkeley, was 18. She married Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury on 6 September 1425, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 14 June 1467, in St Paul's Cathedral, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Beauchamp est née en 1404, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Beauchamp, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Berkeley, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury le 6 septembre 1425, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 juin 1467, à St Paul's Cathedral, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

September 1425 in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 14. Juni 1467 im Alter von 63 Jahren in St Paul's Cathedral, London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Beauchamp(이)가 1404년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Beauchamp(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Berkeley(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1425년 9월 6일 영국 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 6월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 폴'스 캐테드럴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Beauchamp родилась в 1404 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard Beauchamp было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Berkeley, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Talbot 1st Earl of Shrewsbury 6 сентября 1425 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 14 июня 1467 г. в местности ст поул'с кэтедраль, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
